issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The default focus outline is removed using `outline: none` on the `:focus-visible` pseudo-class. While a custom style is intended, the removal of the native indicator on the base `:focus` state without a fallback for all browsers can leave keyboard users without a visual cue.
issuesFound.fix: Line 284: `:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static`, `width: auto`, and `height: auto`, which fails to hide the element visually. This causes screen-reader-only text to appear on the screen, cluttering the UI for sighted users and failing the purpose of a skip-link or hidden label utility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 315: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which reduces the space between words. This significantly degrades readability for users with dyslexia or low vision by causing words to visually run together.
issuesFound.fix: Line 168: `word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `letter-spacing: -0.05em`. Tightening the tracking across the entire document makes character recognition more difficult for many users, violating basic readability principles.
issuesFound.fix: Line 169: `letter-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient line-height on paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: The `p` selector sets `line-height: 1`. This is significantly below the WCAG-recommended minimum of 1.5 for body text, causing lines of text to overlap or become difficult to track for users with cognitive or visual disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 188: `line-height: 1.5;`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.skip-link` uses a `transform: translateY(-200%)` and a transition on `transform`. There is no override in the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block to disable this movement for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 378: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The variable `--colour-text-secondary` is set to `#aaaaaa`. When used against the default background `--colour-bg-page` (`#ffffff`), the contrast ratio is 2.3:1, which is well below the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 55: `--colour-text-secondary: #707070;`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The `a` selector removes text decoration (`text-decoration: none`) globally. In body text (such as inside `.prose` or paragraphs), this leaves colour as the only way to distinguish links from surrounding text, which fails for users with colour blindness.
issuesFound.fix: Line 194: `a { text-decoration: underline; text-underline-offset: 2px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-sm` class sets a `min-height: 20px`. This is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum requirement of 24px for touch targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 541: `min-height: 24px;`

issuesFound.title: Font-size below 16px for body text
issuesFound.explanation: The `html` element sets `font-size: 12px`. This makes the base text size for the entire document too small for comfortable reading, requiring users to zoom immediately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 148: `font-size: 16px;`

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions for the mobile navigation drawer update CSS classes and scroll lock state but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the menu state has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1145: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` Line 1159: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` function changes the layout mode between grid and list but does not update the `aria-pressed` state on the buttons. While it updates a custom attribute, it fails to use the standard ARIA state to communicate the toggle condition to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1865: `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'true'); (and set 'false' on siblings)`

issuesFound.title: dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the `applyFilters` function updates the visible grid and count badge, but it does not explicitly send a summary announcement to a live region. Users may be unaware that the list of products has changed or been filtered out.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1815: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory));`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module updates the error message text and visibility but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field itself. Screen readers will not identify the field as being in an error state during navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2615: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, !result.valid);`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `clearField` function resets the error message and CSS classes but does not remove or reset the `aria-invalid` attribute. A field previously marked as invalid will continue to be announced as such even after being cleared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2685: `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function identifies the trigger and drawer but does not explicitly set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger. This can lead to an undefined state for screen readers until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1215: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions for the mobile navigation toggle the `is-open` class and body scroll lock but do not update the `aria-expanded` state on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified whether the navigation menu is currently expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1545: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` Line 1559: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` function changes the layout mode between grid and list views but does not update the `aria-pressed` state of the buttons. Users of assistive technology will not know which view mode is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2215: `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'true'); // and set 'false' on the sibling button`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onSortChange` function updates the sort order of the product grid, which significantly changes the visual layout and meaning of the list, but no announcement is made to screen readers that the sort has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2181: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.sortKey));`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input

issuesFound.fix: Line 1080: .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible

issuesFound.fix: Line 1120: .footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px. With padding, the total interactive area may be below the recommended 44×44px minimum for touch targets, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 372: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-5); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px. With padding, the total interactive area may be below the recommended 44×44px minimum for touch targets, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 380: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3); }

issuesFound.fix: Line 506: #search-input { height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-10) var(--space-3) var(--space-4); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and clip: auto, making content visible on screen. Screen reader users may encounter redundant or confusing content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 278: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:active transform (translateY) has no reduced-motion override. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience unnecessary movement, which can cause discomfort.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 328: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img transform and transition have no reduced-motion override. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience unnecessary movement, which can cause discomfort.
issuesFound.fix: Line 624: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and reduces readability, particularly for users with reading or visual disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 104: body { word-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The base a selector removes underlines without providing a non-colour visual differentiator. Users with colour blindness or low vision may not distinguish links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 124: a { text-decoration: underline; text-decoration-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions change the visible state of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified of the menu state change, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1795: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_open = true;`. Line 1809: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle (switch) changes between monthly/annual pricing but does not set aria-pressed on the toggle element. Screen reader users cannot perceive the current toggle state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2735: add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', period === 'year');` inside _syncToggleState function. Line 2738: ensure the same line is called when setPeriod runs.

issuesFound.title: Product filter updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates visible card counts and grid state but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users with visual impairments won't know filtering results.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2299: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : 'Filter applied');` after the emit call.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function marks fields invalid but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen readers won't announce the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3404: add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the invalid block. Line 3407: add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` inside the valid block.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers not initialized with aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets up dropdown panels but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on trigger buttons. Screen readers may report an incorrect initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1866: add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after setting aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() and close() functions change answer visibility but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot track expand/collapse state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3124: add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `item.open = true;`. Line 3139: add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `item.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel dynamic slide change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.goTo() function changes the visible slide but only announces via a generic live region string. The announcement does not include the slide content or context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3299: replace the liveRegion.announce call with `liveRegion.announce(_slides[target].label + ', ' + (target + 1) + ' of ' + _slides.length);`.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form submission status not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletterForm._showSuccess() and _showError() functions update visual status but rely on a static live region announcement. The success/error messages are not dynamically announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3550: add `liveRegion.announce(msg);` after _statusEl update. Line 3558: add `liveRegion.announceAssertive(msg);` after _statusEl update.

issuesFound.title: Contact form error summary not programmatically focused
issuesFound.explanation: The contactForm._renderErrorSummary() creates an error list but does not move keyboard focus to the summary container. Keyboard users must manually navigate to the errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3624: add `_errorSummaryEl.focus();` after removing the hidden attribute.

issuesFound.title: Multi-step wizard step change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The multiStepWizard._goToStep() function changes the active step panel but only announces a generic step change. Screen reader users may not know the new step's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3768: replace the liveRegion.announce call with `liveRegion.announce('Step ' + (_current + 1) + ' of ' + _steps.length + ': ' + entering.title);`.

fpTitles: Multi-step wizard step change not announced

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot perceive the menu state, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3000: In the open function, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_open = true;`.
    Line 3020: In the close function, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open and close functions expand/collapse FAQ answers but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger buttons. Screen reader users cannot track the state of FAQ items, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4500: In the open function, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`.
    Line 4520: In the close function, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `item.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._onToggleClick function switches between monthly and annual billing but does not set aria-pressed on the toggle button. Screen reader users cannot perceive which billing period is selected, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3800: In _onToggleClick, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', next === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');` after `const next = ...;`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic search results update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._fetchAndRender function updates search suggestions without announcing the result count via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not informed when search results change, violating WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).
issuesFound.fix: Line 3600: After rendering suggestions, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.searchResultsFound(suggestions.length, query));` or similar.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function marks fields invalid but does not set aria-invalid on the input elements. Screen reader users are not alerted to validation errors, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4800: In the invalid branch, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. In the valid branch, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialized on page load for closed dropdowns
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.init function does not set aria-expanded="false" on dropdown trigger buttons that are initially closed. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3250: In the init loop, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` for each closed dropdown.

issuesFound.title: Click event listener on non-button element without keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop._onClick function is attached to a button via click, but the button also needs a keydown handler for Enter and Space to be fully keyboard accessible. Users who navigate via keyboard cannot activate the button, violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2900: Add `_btn.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') _onClick(e); });` after the click listener.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic price update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates price displays without announcing the billing period change via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the price change, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3700: After updating prices, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged(period));` or similar.

fpTitles: Click event listener on non-button element without keyboard handler

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions in the dropdownMenu module change the visibility of a panel but do not set `aria-expanded` on the associated trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the expanded/collapsed state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1848: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and Line 1851: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncTabAriaStates` function for product filter tabs updates CSS classes but does not set `aria-pressed` on the active filter button. Screen reader users cannot identify which filter is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2358: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `applyFilters` function updates the visible product grid but does not announce the change via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not informed when filter results change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2369: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory));`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the inlineValidator module adds error messages but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid input fields. Screen reader users may not be alerted to validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2790: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and Line 2793: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function removes the error class but does not clear the `aria-invalid` attribute when a field becomes valid. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2793: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's `init` function does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the hamburger trigger button when the drawer is closed on page load. Screen reader users start with an unknown state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1890: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Icon-only button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle IconButton component lacks an aria-label prop, which is required for icon-only interactive elements. Screen reader users will hear an empty or generic "button" announcement without understanding its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1009: <IconButton onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle" aria-label={LABELS[theme]}>

issuesFound.fix: Line 1085: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover rule applies a translateY transform with a transition, but no reduced‑motion media query disables it, which can be problematic for users preferring reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 540: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing on body text
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing: -0.05em, a negative value that can reduce readability for users with dyslexia or low vision, violating WCAG 2.4.8.
issuesFound.fix: Line 115: body { letter-spacing: 0; }

Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card:hover
Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are given `aria‑haspopup="true"` but the script never updates `aria‑expanded` when the panel opens or closes. Assistive technologies therefore cannot convey the open/closed state of the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers missing aria‑expanded state changes
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items set `aria-controls` and `aria-labelledby` but never update `aria‑expanded` on the question button when an answer panel is shown or hidden. This prevents screen‑reader users from knowing which FAQ is open.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search combobox input missing aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The search `<input>` is given `aria‑autocomplete="list"` and `aria‑controls` pointing at the suggestion list, but the script never sets `aria‑expanded` to reflect whether the list is visible. This violates ARIA combobox patterns and leaves the state ambiguous for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle button controls the navigation drawer (`_drawer`), yet no `aria‑controls` attribute is added to reference the drawer’s ID. Screen readers cannot associate the button with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer has an ID (e.g., `srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer')`) and set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑controls', _drawer.id);`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not update aria‑expanded on state change
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` and `close()` functions add/remove CSS classes but never update the button’s `aria‑expanded` state, leaving the ARIA state out‑of‑sync with the visual state.
issuesFound.fix: In `open()`, call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'true');` and in `close()`, call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons do not update aria‑expanded when toggled
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqAccordion.open(item)` and `faqAccordion.close(item)`, the script expands/collapses the answer panel but never updates the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` attribute, breaking the required ARIA state for accordions.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)`, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'true');`; in `close(item)`, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion lacks live‑region announcements for open/close actions
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing an FAQ item does not announce the change to screen‑reader users, violating the need for dynamic content updates to be announced.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, call `liveRegion.announce(item.open ? STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) : STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question));` (or a suitable message) to a polite live region.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors are not reflected via aria‑invalid on inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` displays error messages and adds error classes, but never sets `aria‑invalid="true"` on the invalid field nor clears it on valid input. Assistive technologies cannot convey the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In `inlineValidator.validate`, after computing `result`, add `setAttr(field, 'aria‑invalid', result.valid ? 'false' : 'true');`. Ensure the attribute is removed or set to `"false"` when the field becomes valid.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation toggle button does not update aria‑expanded on state change
FAQ accordion trigger buttons do not update aria‑expanded when toggled

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger` in `mobileNav`) never receives an `aria‑expanded` attribute when the navigation drawer opens or closes, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.open` set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `mobileNav.close` set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing initial aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: On page load the toggle button does not have `aria‑expanded="false"` reflecting its collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.init` after locating `_trigger`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger buttons missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu` the trigger elements are given `aria‑haspopup` but never receive `aria‑expanded` changes when panels open or close, so assistive technologies cannot convey the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance` call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_closeInstance` call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger buttons missing initial aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: Triggers are not given `aria‑expanded="false"` on page load, causing an undefined state for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In `dropdownMenu.init` after creating each instance, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items set `aria‑controls` but never update `aria‑expanded` when answers are shown or hidden, so the expanded state is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: In `faqAccordion.open` call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `faqAccordion.close` call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons missing initial aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: Triggers are not given `aria‑expanded="false"` on page load, leaving the initial state ambiguous.
issuesFound.fix: In `faqAccordion.init` after parsing each item, set `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` for items that start closed.

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle button missing aria‑pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle (`_toggleEl`) updates visual state but never sets `aria‑pressed` to reflect on/off status, violating ARIA button patterns.
issuesFound.fix: In `_onToggleClick` after toggling, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle button missing initial aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: On load the toggle lacks `aria‑pressed`, so its state is not conveyed.
issuesFound.fix: In `pricing.init` after determining the initial period, set `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate` marks fields as invalid visually but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the input, so screen readers do not know the field is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In `inlineValidator._renderResult` when `!result.valid`, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not clear aria‑invalid on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, `inlineValidator._renderResult` removes error text but does not remove or set `aria-invalid="false"`, leaving the invalid state lingering for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In the same function, when `result.valid`, call `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid')` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false')`.

fpTitles: Dropdown menu trigger buttons missing initial aria‑expanded state
FAQ accordion trigger buttons missing initial aria‑expanded state
Form validation does not clear aria‑invalid on valid input

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The <FormGroup> component renders a <label> without an htmlFor attribute, so screen‑reader users cannot associate the label with its input. This affects every form field that uses <FormGroup>.
issuesFound.fix:
Add htmlFor={htmlFor} to the <label> element.  
Line 462: `<label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup does not associate error message via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: When <FormGroup> receives errorId and errorMessage, the corresponding <ErrorMessage> is rendered but the input is not linked to it with aria-describedby, so assistive technologies will not announce the error. This impacts each field that can display an error.
issuesFound.fix: Pass aria-describedby to the input using errorId. Example fix in the field component: `aria-describedby={errorId}`.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button controls the submenu visibility but does not expose its expanded/collapsed state via aria‑expanded, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state. Affects each navigation item with a submenu.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `aria-expanded={isOpen}` to the button.  
Line 219: `aria-expanded={isOpen}`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav submenu toggle button missing aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: Each mobile navigation submenu is toggled by a button that lacks aria‑expanded, so the expanded state is not announced. Affects every mobile navigation item with children.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `aria-expanded={isExpanded}` where isExpanded is the boolean state for that section.  
Line 311: `aria-expanded={isExpanded}`

issuesFound.title: Active desktop navigation link missing aria‑current attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link is indicated only by a CSS class `nav-link--current`; it does not have aria‑current="page", so assistive technologies cannot identify the active page. Affects the single current navigation item.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `aria-current="page"` to the <a> when item.current is true.  
Line 196: `aria-current="page"`

issuesFound.title: Active mobile navigation link missing aria‑current attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation link for the current page uses a CSS class but lacks aria‑current, preventing AT from recognizing the active page.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `aria-current="page"` to the <a> when item.current is true.  
Line 358: `aria-current="page"`

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: Each filter tab button indicates selection only via CSS; there is no aria‑pressed or aria‑selected attribute, so AT cannot tell which filter is active. All filter buttons are affected.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}` to each button.  
Line 553: `aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}`

issuesFound.title: IconButton instances missing required aria‑label (High severity)
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria‑label for accessible naming, but several usages omit it, resulting in icon‑only buttons with no accessible name. Affects the mobile‑nav close button, the theme toggle, and the carousel prev/next buttons.
issuesFound.fix:
Provide an appropriate aria‑label prop for each IconButton.  
Line 236: `<IconButton aria-label="Close navigation panel" …>`  
Line 388: `<IconButton aria-label="Toggle theme" …>`  
Line 822: `<IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" …>`  
Line 1062: `<IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" …>`

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay <div> clickable without role or keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay <div className="modal‑overlay"> closes the dialog on click but is not a native interactive element and lacks role="button", tabIndex, and onKeyDown handling, making it inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix:
Change the <div> to a <button or add role="button" tabIndex={0} and an onKeyDown handler that triggers onClose on Enter/Space.  
Line 1245: `<button type="button" className="modal-overlay" aria-label="Close dialog" onClick={onClose}>`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `.btn:focus-visible` removes the default focus outline with `outline: none` but does not provide a visible alternative focus style on the same rule. This violates WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible, preventing keyboard users from tracking focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1030: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `.btn-sm` sets `min-height: 20px`, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments to interact with the element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1100: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` utility class incorrectly uses `position: static` and `clip: auto`, which does not hide content visually while keeping it accessible. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 and 4.1.2 by removing content from screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `.hero-image img` has a hover animation (`transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(-3deg) rotateX(1deg)`) but the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block does not override this animation. This violates WCAG 2.3.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1210: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes underlines from all links, and no alternative visual differentiator (e.g., font-weight, border-bottom) is provided on `:hover` or `:focus`. This violates WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 300: `a { text-decoration: underline; }`
  Alternatively, if underlines are undesirable, add:
  `a:hover, a:focus { text-decoration: underline; }` or `a { border-bottom: 1px solid transparent; } a:hover, a:focus { border-bottom-color: currentColor; }`

issuesFound.explanation: The `a { text-decoration: none; }` rule removes underlines from all links, relying solely on colour to distinguish them from surrounding text. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color).

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle (`.nav-toggle`) is a `<div>` styled as a button but lacks keyboard event handlers for Enter/Space. Users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot open/close the mobile menu.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` and `close()` functions in the mobile navigation controller do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`.nav-toggle`). Screen readers will not announce the state change.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger (`.nav-toggle`) does not have `aria-expanded="false"` set on page load. Screen readers may not announce the initial closed state.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation state change ("Navigation menu opened" / "Navigation menu closed") is not announced to screen readers via a live region. Users who rely on screen readers will not receive feedback.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle (`.pricing-toggle`) does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute when toggled. Screen readers will not announce the state change between monthly and annual billing.

issuesFound.title: aria-pressed not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle (`.pricing-toggle`) does not have `aria-pressed` set on page load. Screen readers may not announce the initial state.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField()` function does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid form fields. Screen readers will not announce the error state.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField()` function does not clear `aria-invalid` when a field becomes valid. Screen readers will continue to announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: Element with aria-hidden="true" contains focusable children
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie consent banner (`.cookie-banner`) has `aria-hidden="true"` set during fade-out animation but contains focusable buttons. Keyboard users can focus on invisible elements.

issuesFound.title: aria-live region not injected when missing
issuesFound.explanation: The application injects a live region (`#status-region`) if `CONFIG.accessibility.injectLiveRegion` is true, but the live region is not present in the DOM at the time of audit. Dynamic announcements may fail if the live region is not available.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing role
issuesFound.explanation: The file upload zone (`.upload-zone`) does not have an explicit `role="button"` set. Screen readers may not announce it as interactive.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The file upload zone (`.upload-zone`) does not have an accessible name set via `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`. Screen readers will not announce its purpose.

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none but does not provide a visible alternative like box-shadow, border, or a new outline color. This hides the focus ring for keyboard users across the entire site, making navigation impossible for those who do not use a mouse.

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector explicitly removes the outline without defining a box-shadow or border. Keyboard users tabbing to buttons will see no indication of which element is focused, violating WCAG 2.4.7.

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. This critical interactive element becomes invisible to keyboard users when focused, blocking access to the mobile menu.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation links focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The footer nav links remove the outline on focus without providing a visible alternative. Users navigating the footer via keyboard cannot identify the active link, failing WCAG 2.4.7.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which leaves elements visible on screen instead of hiding them visually for screen readers only. This exposes helper text to all users and breaks the intended accessibility pattern.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em and letter-spacing: -0.05em. Negative spacing reduces character and word separation, making text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or low vision (WCAG 1.4.12).

issuesFound.title: Reduced motion media query is empty for skip link
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block exists but contains no CSS rules. It fails to disable the transform transition on .skip-link, causing motion for users who requested reduced motion (WCAG 2.3.3).

issuesFound.title: Reduced motion media query is empty for hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for .hero-image img exists but has empty rule bodies. It fails to disable the transform transition, causing unwanted motion for sensitive users.

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle touch target too small
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle button has a min-height and min-width of 28px, which is below the recommended 44x44px touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8). This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Search button touch target too small
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has explicit width and height of 28px, below the 44px minimum. This creates a small hit area that is difficult to tap accurately.

issuesFound.title: Social links touch target too small
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector sets min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended target size. This makes social icons difficult to activate for touch users.

issuesFound.title: Input hint text has insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The .input-hint class uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a light background (#ffffff or #f8fafc). The contrast ratio is approximately 1.9:1, which fails the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text (WCAG 1.4.3).

issuesFound.title: Stat label text has insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label text uses rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) on a blue gradient background (#5588bb). This results in extremely low contrast, making the text unreadable for many users (WCAG 1.4.3).

issuesFound.title: Footer links lack underline on hover
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links remove text-decoration and only change color on hover. Users with color vision deficiencies may not perceive the hover state, violating WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color).

fpTitles: Reduced motion media query is empty for hero image
Footer links lack underline on hover

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class does not hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static with width: auto and height: auto, which means content is NOT visually hidden. Screen reader users will see duplicate content that sighted users also see. This breaks the intended skip-link and sr-only heading functionality.

issuesFound.title: Body text font-size below 16px readability threshold
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px which cascades to body text. WCAG recommends 16px (1rem) minimum for body text to ensure readability for users with low vision or cognitive disabilities. This makes all descendant text smaller than accessible defaults.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing on body impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em which reduces space between words. This can significantly impair readability for users with dyslexia, cognitive disabilities, or low vision who rely on clear word boundaries to parse text.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block sets outline: none on :focus-visible without any visible alternative. In Windows High Contrast Mode, this removes the focus indicator entirely since custom colors are overridden and no system border is specified.

issuesFound.title: Search input touch target below 44px minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input has height: 28px which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum touch target size of 44×44px (SC 2.5.8). Users with motor impairments or who use touch screens may have difficulty activating this input accurately.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button touch target below 44px minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has width: 28px and height: 28px, both below the 44×44px minimum touch target size. This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments or on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Navigation menu links touch target below 44px minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu > li > a and #nav-menu > li > button selectors set min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended touch target size. Navigation links are critical interactive elements that should be easily activatable by all users.

issuesFound.title: Icon-only button touch target below 44px minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, significantly below the 44×44px minimum touch target size. Icon buttons are often harder to identify and activate, making adequate size even more critical.

issuesFound.title: Filter tabs button touch target below 44px minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button selector sets min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended touch target size. These buttons control product filtering, a critical interactive function that should be easily activatable.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button touch target below 44px minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button selector sets min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended touch target size. These buttons control billing period selection, affecting pricing information shown to users.

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transform transition with rotateY and rotateX effects. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 833-836 is empty, so the animation continues for users who prefer reduced motion, potentially causing vestibular discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Feature card hover transform animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card:hover selector applies transform: translateY(-2px) motion. While there is a prefers-reduced-motion block at line 1019, it only targets .feature-card:hover transform, but the transition property on the base .feature-card (line 1007-1011) still animates on other state changes without reduction.

issuesFound.title: FAQ button chevron rotation animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button::after has transform: rotate(90deg) with transition on transform. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block only disables the transition property but does not reset the transform itself, potentially leaving rotated state without animation control.

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without consistent non-colour differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. While some links have hover underlines, the default state relies on colour alone to distinguish links from surrounding text, failing WCAG 1.4.1 for users who cannot perceive colour differences.

issuesFound.title: Limited forced-colors/high-contrast mode support
issuesFound.explanation: Only two selectors have @media (forced-colors: active) overrides (:focus-visible and .skip-link). Interactive elements like buttons, form inputs, navigation links, and focus states throughout the stylesheet lack high-contrast mode support, making them potentially invisible in Windows High Contrast Mode.
issuesFound.fix: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet) Add @media (forced-colors: active) blocks for: .btn, .btn-primary, .btn-secondary, input, select, textarea, #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button, .filter-tabs button, .pricing-toggle button, .product-card a, .feature-card a, footer nav a

fpTitles: Hero image transform animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
Link underlines removed without consistent non-colour differentiator

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions toggle the CSS classes and scroll lock but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the _trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed when the navigation menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line: mobileNav.open(): setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav fails to activate focus trap on open
issuesFound.explanation: The _trap variable is declared but never initialized with createFocusTrap() in init(), and activate() is never called in open(). Keyboard focus is not moved into the drawer, trapping users outside the modal content.
issuesFound.fix: Line: mobileNav.init(): _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded to 'false', but the open() function fails to set it to 'true'. This creates a state mismatch where the accordion appears expanded visually but reports collapsed to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line: faqAccordion.open(): setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation, markInvalid adds an aria-describedby reference but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line: srUtils.markInvalid(): field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, markValid removes the error description but does not remove or set aria-invalid to 'false'. The field may remain announced as invalid to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line: srUtils.markValid(): field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.auditImages treats all missing alt text as decorative
issuesFound.explanation: The function automatically sets alt="" on any image missing an alt attribute. This incorrectly marks informative images (e.g., product photos) as decorative, hiding them from screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: srUtils.auditImages(): // Remove auto-fix. Log error instead: warn(`srUtils.auditImages: missing alt attribute`, img);

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The function updates the textContent of _countBadge dynamically, but the element lacks aria-live. Users are not notified when the product count changes after filtering.
issuesFound.fix: Line: productFilter.init(): setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-live', 'polite');

issuesFound.title: pricing savings message updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The function updates the textContent of _savingsMsg dynamically without an aria-live region. Users are not notified when the billing period changes and the savings message updates.
issuesFound.fix: Line: pricing.init(): setAttr(_savingsMsg, 'aria-live', 'polite');

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard progress bar lacks role enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function updates aria-valuenow and aria-valuetext but does not ensure the _progressBar element has role="progressbar". If the HTML omits the role, the element is not exposed as a progress bar.
issuesFound.fix: Line: multiStepWizard._updateProgress(): setAttr(_progressBar, 'role', 'progressbar');

fpTitles: srUtils.auditImages treats all missing alt text as decorative
multiStepWizard progress bar lacks role enforcement

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid fails to set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function sets aria-describedby to link the error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, preventing users from understanding the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1673: function markInvalid(field, errorEl) { if (!isElement(field) || !isElement(errorEl)) return; addDescribedBy(field, errorEl); field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid fails to clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the error description but fails to set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. The field may remain stuck in an invalid state for assistive technology even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1688: function markValid(field, errorEl) { if (!isElement(field)) return; field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false'); if (isElement(errorEl) && errorEl.id) { ... } }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open function toggles the navigation drawer visibility and classes but does not update aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2374: function open(announceToSR) { if (_open || !_trigger || !_drawer) return; _open = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); ... }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The close function hides the navigation drawer but does not update aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2394: function close(announceToSR) { if (!_open) return; _open = false; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); ... }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The init function locates the trigger element but does not initialize aria-expanded="false". The trigger's state is undefined until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2433: function init() { if (_initialised) return; _trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle); ... if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function reveals the dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. Assistive technology users are not notified of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2573: function _openInstance(inst) { if (inst.open) return; inst.open = true; setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'); ... }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function hides the dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. Assistive technology users are not notified of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2583: function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) { if (!inst.open) return; inst.open = false; setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open); ... }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The register function sets aria-haspopup but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. The initial state is not exposed to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2648: function register(trigger, panel) { ... setAttr(trigger, 'aria-haspopup', 'true'); setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _open function sets the search state to open but does not update aria-expanded="true" on the input element. Screen readers will not announce that the suggestion list is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3274: function _open() { if (_searchState.open) return; _searchState.open = true; setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _close function sets the search state to closed but does not update aria-expanded="false" on the input element. Screen readers will not announce that the suggestion list is collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3284: function _close() { if (!_searchState.open) return; _searchState.open = false; setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); suggestionListRenderer.hide(); ... }

issuesFound.title: siteSearch.init missing initial aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus indicator for all interactive elements globally, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative, leaving keyboard users unable to see focus on the search button.

issuesFound.title: Table wrapper focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .table-wrapper:focus-visible selector removes the outline without a replacement. Since the wrapper is keyboard-focusable (for scrollable regions), users cannot see when it has focus.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative, preventing keyboard users from seeing focus on accordion triggers.

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size. This makes the button difficult or impossible for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Icon-only button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum. Icon buttons are critical interactive elements and must meet minimum touch target requirements.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox input touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum. Checkboxes are interactive elements that must meet minimum target size requirements.

issuesFound.title: Standard button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is above the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended target size. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating the button.

issuesFound.title: Large button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-lg class sets min-height: 36px, which is below the 44px recommended target size. Despite being "large", it does not meet the recommended minimum for comfortable activation.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle class sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended target size. This is a critical control for mobile navigation and must be easy to activate.

issuesFound.title: Primary navigation menu items touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu > li > a and #nav-menu > li > button selectors set min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended target size. Navigation items must be easy to activate for all users.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown submenu items touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a selector sets min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended target size. Dropdown menu items must meet target size requirements.

issuesFound.title: Site search input touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input selector sets height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended target size. Search inputs are frequently used and must be easy to target.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] selector sets width: 28px and height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended target size.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button selector sets min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended target size. Filter controls must be easy to activate.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle buttons touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button selector sets min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended target size. Pricing toggle buttons are important controls that must meet target size requirements.

issuesFound.title: Product card button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-body .btn-secondary selector sets min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended target size.

issuesFound.title: Social links touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector sets min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended target size. Social media links in the footer must be easy to activate.

issuesFound.title: Broken screen-reader-only utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which does not hide the content visually. Content intended only for screen readers will be visible to all users, breaking the visual layout and potentially exposing text meant to be hidden.

issuesFound.title: Button transform animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition and defines a transform: translateY(1px) on :active. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 424 is empty, so users who prefer reduced motion still see the button movement animation.

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img includes transform in its transition and defines hover transforms. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 900 is empty, so users who prefer reduced motion still see the 3D perspective animation.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words. This can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and may cause words to run together visually.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing aria-expanded updates

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: pricing switch toggle missing aria-checked

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation marks a field as invalid, the function adds an error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true", preventing screen readers from conveying the invalid state to users.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is marked valid, the function removes the error description but does not clear aria-invalid (set to "false" or remove the attribute), potentially leaving stale invalid states announced by assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in srUtils.markValid after the aria-describedby cleanup block): `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on validation errors
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult function in the inline validator calls srUtils.markInvalid and markValid, but these utility functions only manage aria-describedby. They do not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or clear it on valid fields, so screen readers cannot identify invalid fields programmatically.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3782: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` inside the else block (invalid case) before calling srUtils.markInvalid.
  Line 3786: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` inside the if block (valid case) before calling srUtils.markValid.

issuesFound.title: sr-only utility class is broken and does not hide content

This breaks the intended purpose of screen-reader-only text and will expose content that should be hidden from visual users.

issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing an alternative visible focus indicator such as box-shadow or border change. issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has explicit width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24×24 pixels. Users with motor impairments or using touch screens will have difficulty activating this checkbox.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1298: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has word-spacing: -0.2em which reduces spacing between words by 20%. Negative word-spacing can cause words to appear merged together, impairing readability for users with dyslexia, cognitive disabilities, or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 133: Remove word-spacing: -0.2em; from the body rule, or change to: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; }

issuesFound.title: Base font-size below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px, which is below the recommended minimum of 16px (1rem) for body text. Small base font sizes make text harder to read, particularly for users with low vision, and users who have not set a larger default in their browser.
issuesFound.fix: Line 127: html { scroll-behavior: smooth; text-size-adjust: 100%; -webkit-text-size-adjust: 100%; font-size: 1rem; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); tab-size: 4; -moz-tab-size: 4; height: 100%; }

issuesFound.title: Empty prefers-reduced-motion media query
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty and does not override any animations or transitions. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience all motion effects defined elsewhere in the stylesheet.
issuesFound.fix: Line 386-387: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; scroll-behavior: auto !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Transform transition without reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img element has a transform transition defined but the reduced-motion media query at lines 758-762 contains empty rules that do not disable the transform. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the perspective rotation animation on hover.
issuesFound.fix: Line 758-762: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Transform transition without reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn element includes transform in its transition property and .btn:active applies transform: translateY(1px), but there is no reduced-motion override. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience button movement on click.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 542: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule a { text-decoration: none; } removes underlines from all links. Without underlines, links must be distinguished from surrounding text by more than colour alone (WCAG 1.4.1). The CSS does not provide an alternative visual differentiator such as border-bottom or font-weight for links in body text contexts.
issuesFound.fix: Line 197: Remove the global rule, or add: a { text-decoration: none; } .prose a { text-decoration: underline; text-underline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands the answer panel but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an inconsistent state where closed items are correctly marked but open items are not.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3225: add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria-invalid on fields
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() and markValid() functions only manage aria-describedby references but never set aria-invalid on the input field. Screen reader users cannot programmatically determine which fields have validation errors, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1107: add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`
  Fix: Line 1115: add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` inside the markValid function after the aria-describedby cleanup.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav trigger aria-expanded not initialized at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button when the page loads. The initial state is undefined, so screen readers may announce an unknown expansion state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1575: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown triggers aria-expanded not initialized at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets aria-haspopup but never sets aria-expanded="false" on triggers when they are registered. The initial state is undefined.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1770: add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after the aria-controls assignment.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._updateCountBadge() function updates the visible count text but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users cannot perceive that the filter has changed the number of visible products.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2825: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(count) || \`Showing \${count} products\`);` after the aria-label assignment.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates prices and UI but does not announce the billing period change to screen readers. Users cannot perceive that the pricing display has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3890: add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? (STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing — save 20%') : (STRINGS.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing'));` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown, the function sets aria-haspopup but never initializes aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. The initial collapsed state is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3052: `if (!trigger.hasAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function links the error message via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1933: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error message reference but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. The field may remain announced as invalid even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1940: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters() does not announce filter changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the visible product count changes, there is no live region announcement. Screen reader users are not informed that the product grid has been updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3850: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(activeCategory) || 'Showing ' + visibleCount + ' products');`

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() does not announce billing period changes
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is changed, prices update visually but there is no live region announcement. Screen reader users are not informed that prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4518: `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? (STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing') : (STRINGS.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing'));`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not update aria-expanded on trigger buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show/hide dropdown panels but never set aria-expanded on the trigger element. Screen reader users have no indication of whether dropdowns are expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2820: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs lack aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons act as toggle controls (only one can be active at a time) but _syncTabAriaStates() only toggles CSS classes. Without aria-pressed="true"/"false", screen reader users cannot identify which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3980: replace `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` with `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons lack aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) act as toggle controls but _syncToggleState() only toggles CSS classes. Without aria-pressed, screen reader users cannot identify the current selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4360: add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` inside the `_periodBtns.forEach(btn => {...})` loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on form fields
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function links the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2100: add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` before `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error description reference but does not clear aria-invalid back to "false" or remove it. Fields may retain an invalid state announcement after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2110: add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` after the existing `if (refs.length)` block, or use `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` to clear the attribute entirely.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod does not announce billing period changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes (monthly/annual), prices update visually but there is no live region announcement. Screen reader users are not informed of the pricing change.
